notes: Strongest moment: the turn-9 sunk-cost counterfactual — quoting the user's own effort-based argument back and asking whether the half-built work was doing the persuading — extracted a candid concession that materially sharpened the thesis. Weakest moment: turn 8 stacked three alternatives into one question, slightly diffusing the probe, though it stayed a genuine viewpoints question rather than a suggestion. The examiner correctly ran a falsification probe (turn 10) and treated the user's concrete 5%/1% kill thresholds as a validation strength in the brief, never mislabeling the falsifiable claim as faith-based; verdict of 'sharpened' is well-supported and no solutioning preceded the Phase 4 brief.
